chunk_summary: Risk-stratified screening for pediatric obesity comorbidities includes resource-adapted bundles with actionable follow-ups, emphasizing hypertension, dyslipidemia, glucose metabolism, and liver disease evaluation, especially in high-risk cases.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of a risk-stratified screening bundle for pediatric obesity comorbidities.
Identify resource-adapted strategies for comorbidity screening in low-resource settings.
Learn the specific screening recommendations for hypertension, dyslipidemia, glucose metabolism, and liver disease in children with obesity.
Recognize the importance of documenting post-test actions for abnormal results.
key_facts: Children with obesity aged ≥10 years should be screened for hypertension, dyslipidemia, abnormal glucose metabolism, and steatotic liver disease (IAP, 2023).
Routine blood pressure (BP) measurement is recommended starting at age 3 in children with overweight or obesity.
Baseline screening includes BP at every visit, fasting or non-fasting lipid profile, fasting glucose and/or HbA1c, and ALT with repeat if elevated.
Higher screening intensity is advised for class 2/3 obesity, acanthosis, or strong family history.
In resource-limited settings, prioritize repeated clinic BP and non-fasting lipid screening, with planned fasting tests for high-risk cases.
reference_quote: The 2023 IAP obesity guideline recommends that children with obesity older than 10 years be evaluated for key complications, including hypertension, dyslipidemia, abnormal glucose metabolism, and steatotic liver disease.
document_key_topics: Risk-stratified screening for pediatric obesity comorbidities
Actionable pathways for abnormal test results
Guidelines for hypertension, dyslipidemia, glucose metabolism, and liver disease evaluation
